entries.description: Center for Countering Digital Hate analysis: Over 11-day period (Dec 29, 2025 - Jan 8, 2026), Grok generated approximately 3 million sexualized images, including an estimated 23,000 images depicting apparent children, at a rate of ~190 sexualized images per minute. Sample reviewed: 200,000 images out of 4.6 million produced during period.[citation:7][citation:10]
entries.causation_evidence: Center for Countering Digital Hate report; Bloomberg Law; USA Today

entries.description: Tennessee criminal case: Individual arrested for using Grok to generate explicit content of at least 18 underage girls by manipulating real photos, including school photographs and family pictures. Police notified multiple families that CSAM involving their daughters was produced.[citation:4][citation:10]
entries.causation_evidence: The Tennessean; USA Today; police investigation records

entries.description: Class action lawsuit filed by three Tennessee teenagers in U.S. Northern District of California Court seeking class-action status for individuals across the United States who were 'reasonably identifiable' in sexualized images or videos created by Grok using real images of themselves. Complaint estimates more than 1,000 minors may have had photos manipulated into sexualized images or videos.[citation:1][citation:4]
entries.causation_evidence: Times Now; The Tennessean; court filing

entries.description: Ashley St. Clair, a conservative influencer, filed lawsuit in New York against xAI alleging Grok was used to create sexual images of her, including a photo of her when she was 14 years old.[citation:4][citation:10]
entries.causation_evidence: The Tennessean; USA Today; court filing

entries.description: California Attorney General Rob Bonta announced state Department of Justice investigation into xAI regarding non-consensual intimate images and child sexual abuse material generated by Grok.[citation:4][citation:10]
entries.causation_evidence: The Tennessean; USA Today; California DOJ announcement

entries.description: Anonymous plaintiff filed class action lawsuit in California's U.S. Northern District Court alleging xAI knew Grok would be used to create sexually explicit images of women and girls using deepfake technology and those images can 'never be erased.'[citation:4][citation:10]
entries.causation_evidence: The Tennessean; USA Today; court filing

entries.description: Malaysia and Indonesia became first countries to block Grok due to concerns about it being used to generate sexually explicit and nonconsensual images.[citation:4]

Grok reached 314 million global website visits in January 2026, becoming the third-largest AI chatbot by traffic, surpassing DeepSeek (298.3 million). Independent visitors: 53.8% new users, highest among major chatbots.[citation:2]

Grok reached 1.53 million monthly active users in South Korea in February 2026, ranking third among AI chatbot apps behind ChatGPT (22.93M) and Zeta (4.02M). Total usage time: 2.93 million hours.[citation:5][citation:8]
xAI purchased 522-acre site in Memphis for 'Colossus' data center, described as world's largest AI data center at the time.[citation:9]
entries.causation_evidence: Memphis Chamber of Commerce via Chosunbiz

entries.description: xAI purchased additional 1 million square meter site (approx. 247 acres) in southwest Memphis to expand AI data center infrastructure.[citation:9]
entries.causation_evidence: Memphis Chamber of Commerce via Chosunbiz

entries.description: xAI filed construction permit for new 312,000 sq ft, 4-story building on 79-acre parcel adjacent to Colossus 2 data center in Memphis. $659 million investment.[citation:3][citation:6]
entries.causation_evidence: Data Center Dynamics; Memphis Business Journal via bianews

entries.description: Environmental lawsuit filed against xAI over alleged unpermitted use of gas turbines at Colossus 2 data center in Memphis. Campaigners cite pollution concerns from turbines used to快速 power up sites.[citation:3]

Elon Musk posted on X: 'VHS won in the end, in part because they allowed spicy mode,' comparing Grok's 'spicy mode' (lack of content guardrails) to VHS/Betamax format war and availability of pornographic content. Cited in lawsuits as evidence of deliberate design choice.[citation:10]
entries.causation_evidence: USA Today; X post; court filings
